title: POST-H-012-D — PolicyEngine enforcement homogéneo
commands: python -m pytest -p no:ddtrace tests/test_policy_engine_approval_rbac_enforcement.py tests/test_policy_engine.py tests/test_approval_binding.py tests/test_rbac_exposure.py -q
python -m pytest -p no:ddtrace tests/test_approval_policy_binding.py tests/test_approval_cli.py tests/test_policy_engine.py -q
python -m devpilot_core schema validate --schema-id PostHManifest --instance docs/post_h_012_d_manifest.json --json
pass_criteria: PolicyEngine blocks critical/sensitive actions without valid approval.
PolicyEngine blocks insufficient role for sensitive actions.
PolicyEngine emits normalized findings for approval, RBAC and scope mismatch.
Existing read-only commands remain allowed and deterministic.
block_criteria: PathGuard, SecretGuard, ToolInjectionGuard, PromptInjectionGuard or CostGuard is relaxed.
PolicyEngine becomes non-deterministic or relies on network/API/LLM judge.
Any remote execution, connector write, plugin execution or destructive side effect is enabled.
risks: PolicyEngine enforcement is implemented-initial and the final quality gate remains planned for POST-H-012-E.
Sensitive actions remain non-executable by design even with approval/RBAC metadata.
RBAC taxonomy gaps such as undeclared maintainer role are surfaced and blocked, not silently bypassed.
summary: POST-H-012-D adds homogeneous PolicyEngine enforcement for SensitiveActionCatalog, strong approval binding and RBAC without enabling side effects.
